company.name: RockYou, Inc.
company.business_description: RockYou, Inc. operated a website and online service accessible worldwide on the Internet through RockYou.com where users could develop content, create and share widgets (including slideshows), and register to save content for later retrieval and editing.

case_info.legal_authority: Sections 5(a)(1), 5(m)(1)(A), 13(b), and 16(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 45(a)(1), 45(m)(1)(A), 53(b), and 56(a); Sections 1303(c) and 1306(d) of the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998 (COPPA), 15 U.S.C. §§ 6501-6506, 6502(c) and 6505(d)

complaint.factual_background: RockYou, Inc. operated a website that collected personal information from consumers but failed to implement basic data security measures, resulting in unauthorized access to approximately 32 million email addresses and passwords. Additionally, from December 2008 through January 2010, RockYou knowingly collected personal information from approximately 179,000 children under age 13 without obtaining parental consent or providing proper notice, in violation of COPPA requirements.

representations_made.description: Claimed to use commercially reasonable physical, managerial, and technical safeguards to preserve the integrity and security of personal information
representations_made.quoted_text: RockYou! uses commercially reasonable physical, managerial, and technical safeguards to preserve the integrity and security of your personal information. We cannot, however, ensure or warrant the security of any information you transmit to RockYou! and you do so at your own risk. Once we receive your transmission of information, RockYou! makes commercially reasonable efforts to ensure the security of our systems. However, please note that this is not a guarantee that such information may not be accessed, disclosed, altered, or destroyed by breach of any of our physical, technical, or managerial safeguards. (Exhibit A, www.rockyou.com Privacy Policy, February 13,2006)

representations_made.description: Claimed not to knowingly collect or maintain personally identifiable information from persons under 13 years of age
representations_made.quoted_text: Protecting the privacy of young children is especially important. For that reason, RockYou! does not knowingly collect or maintain personally identifiable information or non-personally-identifiable information on the RockYou! Sites from persons under years of age, and no part of our website is directed to persons under 13. If you are under years of age then please do not use or access the RockYou! Sites at any time or in any manner. If RockYou! learns that personally identifiable information of persons under

representations_made.description: Stated that if RockYou learns that personally identifiable information of persons under 13 has been collected, it will take appropriate steps to delete this information
representations_made.quoted_text: manner. If RockYou! learns that personally identifiable information of persons under years of age has been collected on the RockYou! Sites without verified parental consent, then RockYou! will take appropriate steps to delete this information. Exhibit A (Privacy

counts.title: Data Security Misrepresentation
counts.representation: Defendant represented that it implemented reasonable and appropriate measures to protect against unauthorized access to the personal information it obtained from customers
counts.fact: Defendant did not implement reasonable and appropriate measures to protect against unauthorized access, including: unnecessarily collecting email passwords, storing passwords in clear text, failing to segment servers, and not protecting against SQL injection and XSS attacks
counts.conclusion: Therefore, the representation set forth in Paragraph 15 was false or misleading and constituted a deceptive act or practice in violation of Section 5(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a).

counts.title: COPPA Rule Violations
counts.representation: Defendant collected, used, and/or disclosed personal information from children
counts.fact: Defendant failed to: provide sufficient notice on its website of information practices; provide direct notice to parents; obtain verifiable parental consent; and establish reasonable procedures to protect confidentiality, security, and integrity of children's personal information
counts.conclusion: Therefore, Defendant has violated the Children's Online Privacy Protection Rule, 16 C.F.R. Part 312.

counts.title: Misrepresentation About Children's Information Collection
counts.representation: Defendant represented that it: (a) did not knowingly collect or maintain personal information from children under 13; and (b) would delete any personal information that it learns it has collected from children
counts.fact: Defendant knowingly collected, and did not delete, personal information from children under 13
counts.conclusion: Therefore, the representations set forth in Paragraph 35 above were false and misleading and constitute deceptive acts or practices in violation of Section 5(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a).

definitions.Rule: the Federal Trade Commission's Children's Online Privacy Protection Rule, 16 C.F.R. Part 312.
definitions.child: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.collects: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.collection: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.Commission: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.delete: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.disclosure: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.Internet: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.online contact information: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.operator: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.parent: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.person: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.personal information: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.third party: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.verifiable consent: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.website or online service directed to ch: defined in Section 312.2 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. § 312.2.
definitions.Consumer personal information: individually identifiable information from or about an individual consumer including, but not limited to: (a) a first and last name; (b) a home or other physical address, including street name and name of city or town; (c) an email address or other online contact information, such as an instant messaging user identifier or a screen name; (d) a telephone number; (e) a Social Security number; (f) a driver's license or other state-issued identification number; (g) a financial institution account number; (h) credit or debit card information; (i) a persistent identifier, such as a customer number held in a "cookie," a static Internet Protocol ("IP") address, or processor serial number; (j) nonpublic communications and content posted on Defendant's web site or within Defendant's applications provided on any other web site; or (k) any information that is combined with any of (a) through (j) above.
definitions.Defendant: RockYou, Inc., a corporation, its successors and assigns and its officers, agents, representatives, and employees.

provisions.title: Injunction Against COPPA Violations
provisions.summary: Defendant is enjoined from violating the Children's Online Privacy Protection Rule in connection with any website or online service directed to children.

requirements.description: Must not fail to provide sufficient notice of information collected from children as required by Section 312.4(b) of the Rule
requirements.quoted_text: A. failing to provide sufficient notice of the information Defendant collects online onsent Decree Page 44 of Case3:12-cv-01487-SI Document4 Filed03/28/12 Page8 of 31 11 from children, how it uses such information, its disclosure practices, and all other content, as required by Section 312.4(b) of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. §§ 312.4(b);

requirements.description: Must not fail to provide direct notice to parents as required by Section 312.4(c) of the Rule
requirements.quoted_text: B. failing to provide direct notice to parents of what information Defendant collects 44 online from children, how it uses such information, its disclosure practices, and 55 66 all other required content, as required by Section 312.4(c )) of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. 77

requirements.description: Must not fail to obtain verifiable parental consent before collection, use, or disclosure of personal information from children as required by Section 312.5 of the Rule
requirements.quoted_text: C. failing to obtain verifiable parental consent before any collection, use, and/or 99 disclosure of personal information from children, as required by Section 312.5 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. §§ 312.5(a)(1);

requirements.description: Must not fail to establish and maintain reasonable procedures to protect confidentiality, security, and integrity of personal information collected from children as required by Section 312.8 of the Rule
requirements.quoted_text: D. failing to establish and maintain reasonable procedures to protect the confidentiality, security, and integrity of personal information collected from children, as required by Section 312.8 of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. §§ 312.8; or

requirements.description: Must not violate any other provision of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. Part 312
requirements.quoted_text: E. violating any other provision of the Rule, 16 C.F.R. Part 312, and as the Rule may hereafter be amended. AA copy of the Rule is attached hereto as "Appendix A" and incorporated herein as if fully set forth verbatim.

provisions.title: Prohibition Against Misrepresentations About Children's Information
provisions.summary: Defendant is enjoined from making any misrepresentation concerning the collection, use, disclosure, or deletion of children's personal information.

requirements.description: Must not make any misrepresentation concerning the collection, use, disclosure, or deletion of children's personal information in connection with operation of any website or online service
requirements.quoted_text: 15. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, and its officers, agents, representatives, and employees, and all persons in active concert or participation with them who receive actual notice of this Order by personal service or otherwise, are hereby enjoined, directly or through any corporation, subsidiary, division, website, or other device, in connection with the operation of any website or online service, from making any misrepresentation concerning the collection, use, disclosure, or deletion of children's personal information.

provisions.title: Deletion of Children's Personal Information
provisions.summary: Defendant must delete all personal information collected and maintained in violation of the Rule from April 21, 2000 through the date of entry of the Order.

requirements.description: Delete all personal information collected and maintained in violation of the Rule from April 21, 2000 through date of entry of Order
requirements.quoted_text: 16. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, within 10 days from the date of receipt of 33 notice of the entry of this Order shall delete all personal information collected and 44 maintained within its possession, custody, or control in violation of the Rule at any time 55 66 from April 21, 2000 through the date of entry of this Order.

provisions.title: Consumer Education Remedy
provisions.summary: Defendant must place a clear and conspicuous notice on websites directed to children that provides a hyperlink to FTC privacy tips for 5 years.

requirements.description: Place clear and conspicuous notice on websites directed to children with hyperlink to OnGuardOnline.gov for tips on protecting kids' privacy online
requirements.quoted_text: 17. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that, for aa period of 55 years from the date of entry of this 99 Order, Defendant, in connection with its operation of any website or online service directed to children, and any website or online service through which Defendant, with actual knowledge, collects, uses, and/or discloses personal information from children, shall place aa clear and conspicuous notice, that will unavoidably be seen by users prior to the collection of personal information from the users, which states as follows in bold typeface: NOTICE: Visit www.OnGuardOnline.gov for tips from the Federal Trade Commission on protecting kids' privacy online ["www.OnguardOnline.gov" must contain aa hyperlink to http://www.onguardonline. gov/ topics/kids-privacy .aspx]

requirements.description: Change hyperlinks/URLs within 15 days after receipt of notice from FTC of a change to such hyperlinks/URLs
requirements.quoted_text: Defendant shall be required to change the hyperlinks/URLs within 15 days after receipt of notice from the Federal Trade Commission of aa change to such hyperlinks/URLs.

provisions.title: Civil Penalty
provisions.summary: Defendant must pay a civil penalty of $250,000 to the United States within 5 days of receipt of notice of entry of the Order.

requirements.description: Pay civil penalty of $250,000 by electronic fund transfer
requirements.quoted_text: 18. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant shall pay to Plaintiff aa civil penalty, pursuant to Section 5(m)(1)(A) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 45(m)(1)(A), in the amount of two hundred and fifty thousand dollars ($250,000), due and payable within five (5) 27 days of receipt of notice of the entry of this Order. Unless otherwise directed, payment onsent Decree Page 66 of Case3:12-cv-01487-SI Document4 Filed03/28/12 Page10 of 31 11 shall be made by electronic fund transfer in accordance with procedures specified by the Consumer Protection Branch, Civil Division, U.S. Department of Justice, Washington, 33 DC 20530.

provisions.title: Prohibition Against Misrepresentations About Consumer Information Security
provisions.summary: Defendant must not misrepresent the extent to which it maintains and protects the privacy, confidentiality, security, or integrity of consumer personal information.

requirements.description: Must not misrepresent in any manner the extent to which they maintain and protect the privacy, confidentiality, security, or integrity of consumer personal information
requirements.quoted_text: 2. IT IS ORDERED that Defendant, and its officers, agents, representatives, and employees, directly or through any corporation, subsidiary, limited liability company, division, or other device, in connection with the advertising, marketing, promotion, offering for sale, or sale of any product or service, in or affecting commerce, shall not misrepresent in any manner, expressly or by implication, the extent to which they maintain and protect the privacy, confidentiality, security, or integrity of consumer personal information collected fi-om or about consumers.

provisions.title: Information Security Program
provisions.summary: Defendant must establish and implement a comprehensive information security program to protect the security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal information.

requirements.description: Establish and implement, and thereafter maintain, a comprehensive information security program that is reasonably designed to protect the security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal information
requirements.quoted_text: 3. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, and its officers, agents, representatives, and employees, directly or through any corporation, subsidiary, limited liability 33 company, division, or other device, in connection with the advertising, marketing, 44 promotion, offering for sale, or sale of any product or service, in or affecting commerce, 55 66 shall, no later than the date of service of this order, establish and implement, and 77 thereafter maintain, aa comprehensive information security program that is reasonably 88 designed to protect the security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal 99 information collected from or about consumers. Such program, the content and implementation of which must be fully documented in writing, shall contain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards appropriate to Defendant's size and complexity, the nature and scope of Defendant's activities, and the sensitivity of the consumer personal information collected from or about consumers, including:
requirements.deadline: no later than the date of service

requirements.description: Designate employee(s) to coordinate and be accountable for the information security program
requirements.quoted_text: A. the designation of an employee or employees to coordinate and be accountable for the information security program.

requirements.description: Identify material internal and external risks to security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal information and assess sufficiency of safeguards
requirements.quoted_text: B. the identification of material internal and external risks to the security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal information that could result in the unauthorized disclosure, misuse, loss, alteration, destruction, or other compromise of such information, and assessment of the sufficiency of any safeguards in place to control these risks. At aa minimum, this risk assessment should include consideration of risks in each area of relevant operation, including, but not limited to: (1) employee training and management; (2) information systems, including network and software design, information processing, storage, transmission, and disposal; and (3) prevention, detection, and response to attacks, onsent Decree Page 88 of Case3:12-cv-01487-SI Document4 Filed03/28/12 Page12 of 31 11 intrusions, or other systems failures.

requirements.description: Design and implement reasonable safeguards to control risks identified through risk assessment, and regularly test or monitor effectiveness
requirements.quoted_text: C. the design and implementation of reasonable safeguards to control the 33 risks identified through risk assessment, and regular testing or monitoring 44 of the effectiveness ofthe safeguards' key controls, systems, and 55 66 procedures.

requirements.description: Develop and use reasonable steps to select and retain service providers capable of safeguarding consumer personal information and require service providers by contract to implement and maintain appropriate safeguards
requirements.quoted_text: D. the development and use of reasonable steps to select and retain service providers 88 capable of appropriately safeguarding consumer personal information they 99 receive from Defendant, and requiring service providers by contract to implement and maintain appropriate safeguards.

requirements.description: Evaluate and adjust information security program in light of testing and monitoring results, material changes to operations or business arrangements, or other circumstances that may impact effectiveness
requirements.quoted_text: E. the evaluation and adjustment of Defendant's information security program in light of the results of the testing and monitoring required by subpart C, any material changes to Defendant's operations or business arrangements, or any other circumstances that Defendant knows or has reason to know may have aa material impact on the effectiveness of its information security program.

provisions.title: Third-Party Security Assessments
provisions.summary: Defendant must obtain initial and biennial assessments from a qualified, objective, independent third-party professional for 20 years.

requirements.description: Obtain initial and biennial assessments from qualified, objective, independent third-party professional using procedures and standards generally accepted in the profession
requirements.quoted_text: 4. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that, in connection with its compliance with Paragraph 23 20 of this order, Defendant shall obtain initial and biennial assessments and reports ("Assessments") from aa qualified, objective, independent third-party professional, who uses procedures and standards generally accepted in the profession. The reporting period for the Assessments shall cover: (1) the first year after service of the Order for the initial Assessment, and (2) each 22 year period thereafter for 20 years after service of the Order for the biennial Assessments.

requirements.description: Each Assessment must set forth specific safeguards implemented and maintained, explain their appropriateness, explain how they meet or exceed protections required by Paragraph 23, and certify security program is operating with sufficient effectiveness
requirements.quoted_text: A. Each Assessment shall: 1. set forth the specific administrative, technical, and physical safeguards 33 that Defendant has implemented and maintained during the reporting 44 period; 55 66 2. explain how such safeguards are appropriate to Defendant's size and 77 complexity, the nature and scope of Defendant's activities, and the 88 sensitivity of the consumer personal information collected from or about 99 consumers; 3. explain how the safeguards that have been implemented meet or exceed the protections required by the Paragraph 23 of this Order; and 4. certify that Defendant's security program is operating with sufficient effectiveness to provide reasonable assurance that the security, confidentiality, and integrity of consumer personal information is protected and has so operated throughout the reporting period.

requirements.description: Each Assessment must be prepared and completed within 60 days after the end of the reporting period by a qualified professional (CISSP, CISA, GIAC, or approved person/organization)
requirements.quoted_text: B. Each Assessment shall be prepared and completed within 60 days after the end of the reporting period to which the Assessment applies by aa person qualified as aa Certified Information System Security Professional (CISSP) or as aa Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA); aa person holding Global Information Assurance Certification (GIAC) from the SysAdmin, Audit, Network, Security (SANS) Institute; or aa qualified person or organization approved by the Associate Director for Enforcement, Bureau of Consumer Protection, Federal Trade Commission, Washington, D.C. 20580.

requirements.description: Provide the initial Assessment to the Associate Director for Enforcement within 10 days after the Assessment has been prepared
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant shall provide the initial Assessment to the Associate Director for Enforcement, Bureau of Consumer Protection, Federal Trade Commission, 33 Washington, D.C. 20580, within 10 days after the Assessment has been prepared.

requirements.description: Retain all subsequent biennial Assessments until order is terminated and provide to Associate Director for Enforcement within 10 days of request
requirements.quoted_text: All subsequent biennial Assessments shall be retained by Defendant until the 55 66 order is terminated and provided to the Associate Director for Enforcement within 77 10 days of request.

provisions.title: Order Acknowledgments
provisions.summary: Defendant must obtain acknowledgments of receipt of the Order from itself and deliver the Order to specified personnel and entities.

requirements.description: Submit to Commission an acknowledgment of receipt of Order sworn under penalty of perjury
requirements.quoted_text: A. Defendant, within 77 days of entry of this Order, must submit to the Commission an acknowledgment of receipt of this Order sworn under penalty of perjury.

requirements.description: Deliver a copy of Order to all principals, officers, directors, and managers; all employees, agents, and representatives having supervisory responsibilities relating to collection, retention, storage, or security of consumer personal information and relating to operation of any website or online service subject to Order; and any business entity resulting from any change in structure
requirements.quoted_text: B. For 88 years after entry ofthis Order, Defendant must deliver aa copy of this Order to: (1) all principals, officers, directors, and managers; (2) all employees, agents, and representatives having supervisory responsibilities relating to the collection, retention, storage, or security of consumer personal information and all employees, agents, and representatives having supervisory responsibilities related to the operation of any website or online service subject to this Order; and (3) any 22 business entity resulting from any change in structure as set forth in the Section 23 titled "Compliance Reporting." Delivery must occur within 77 days of entry of this Order for current personnel. To all others, delivery must occur before they assume their responsibilities.

requirements.description: Obtain, within 30 days, a signed and dated acknowledgment of receipt of Order from each individual or entity to which Defendant delivered a copy of the Order
requirements.quoted_text: C. From each individual or entity to which aa Defendant delivered aa copy of this Order, that Defendant must obtain, within 30 days, aa signed and dated acknowledgment of receipt of this Order.

provisions.title: Compliance Reporting
provisions.summary: Defendant must make timely submissions to the Commission including a comprehensive compliance report 180 days after entry of the Order and change notifications for 20 years.

requirements.description: Submit a compliance report, sworn under penalty of perjury, 180 days after date of entry of Order with contact information, business identification, business activities, compliance status, registration process details, privacy notices, parental consent methods, and Order Acknowledgments
requirements.quoted_text: A. One hundred eighty (180) days after the date of entry of this Order, Defendant 55 66 must submit aa compliance report, sworn under penalty of perjury. This report 77 must: 88 1. Designate at least one telephone number and an email, physical, and 99 postal address as points of contact, which representatives of the Commission may use to communicate with Defendant; 2. Identify all of Defendant's businesses by all of their names, telephone numbers, and physical, postal, email, and Internet addresses; 3. Describe the activities of each business, including the products and services offered and the means of advertising, marketing, and sales; 4. Describe in detail whether and how Defendant is in compliance with each Section of this Order; 5. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail the criteria and process through which Defendant's websites or online services register visitors online for any activity requiring the submission of personal information, and aa copy of each different version of screen or page providing or collecting registration information; 6. Provide aa copy of each different version of any privacy notice posted on each website or online service operated by Defendant; 7. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail each place where the privacy onsent Decree Page of Case3:12-cv-01487-SI Document4 Filed03/28/12 Page16 of 31 11 notice on any such website or online service is located and aa copy of each different version of screen or page on which such website or online 33 service collects personal information; 44 8. Provide aa copy of each different version of any privacy notice sent to 55 66 parents of children that register on each website or online service; 77 9. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail when and how each such notice 88 to parents is provided; 99 10. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail the methods used to obtain verifiable parental consent prior to any collection, use, and/or disclosure of personal information from children; 11. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail the means provided for parents to review the personal information collected from their children and to refuse to permit its further use or maintenance; 12. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail why each type of information 00 18 collected from aa child is reasonably necessary for the provision of the particular related activity; 13. Provide aa statement setting forth in detail the procedures used to protect the confidentiality, security, and integrity of personal information collected from children; and 14. Provide aa copy of each Order Acknowledgement obtained pursuant to this Order, unless previously submitted to the Commission.

requirements.description: Submit a compliance notice, sworn under penalty of perjury, within 14 days of any change in designated point of contact or structure of Defendant or any entity that Defendant has ownership interest in or controls that may affect compliance obligations
requirements.quoted_text: B. For 20 years following entry of this Order, Defendant must submit aa compliance notice, sworn under penalty of perjury, within 14 days of any change in the onsent Decree Page of Case3:12-cv-01487-SI Document4 Filed03/28/12 Page17 of 31 11 following: (a) any designated point of contact; or (b) the structure of Defendant or any entity that Defendant has any ownership interest in or directly or indirectly 33 controls that may affect compliance obligations arising under this Order, 44 including: creation, merger, sale, or dissolution of the entity or any subsidiary, 55 66 parent, or affiliate that engages in any acts or practices subject to this Order.

requirements.description: Submit notice of filing of any bankruptcy petition, insolvency proceeding, or similar proceeding by or against Defendant within 14 days of its filing
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant must submit to the Commission notice of the filing of any bankruptcy 88 petition, insolvency proceeding, or any similar proceeding by or against 99 Defendant within 14 days of its filing.

provisions.title: Recordkeeping
provisions.summary: Defendant must create certain records for 20 years after entry of the Order, and retain each such record for 5 years.

requirements.description: Maintain accounting records showing revenues from all goods or services sold, costs incurred, and resulting net profit or loss
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant must create certain records for 20 years after entry of the Order, and to retain each such record for 55 years. Specifically,

requirements.description: Maintain personnel records showing for each person providing services their name, addresses, telephone numbers, job title or position, dates of service, and if applicable, reason for termination
requirements.quoted_text: A. Accounting records showing the revenues from all goods or services sold, all 44 costs incurred in generating those revenues, and the resulting net profit or loss;

requirements.description: Maintain a copy of all complaints submitted by consumers regarding information security practices or practices relating to collection or retention of consumer personal information
requirements.quoted_text: B. Personnel records showing, for each person providing services, whether as an 77 employee or otherwise, that person's: name, addresses, and telephone numbers; 88 job title or position; dates of service; and, if applicable, the reason for 99 termination;

requirements.description: Maintain all records necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of Order, including all submissions to the Commission
requirements.quoted_text: C. AA copy of all complaints submitted by consumers to Defendant regarding its information security practices or its practices relating to the collection or retention of consumer personal information, including from children;

requirements.description: Maintain a sample copy of every materially different form, page, or screen through which Defendant collects personal information, and sample copy of each materially different document containing any representation regarding collection, use, and disclosure practices pertaining to personal information of a child
requirements.quoted_text: D. All records necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including all submissions to the Commission; and

provisions.title: Compliance Monitoring
provisions.summary: The Commission is authorized to monitor compliance with this Order through various means including requests for reports, depositions, document production, interviews, and other lawful discovery methods.

requirements.description: Within 14 days of receipt of written request from Commission representative, Defendant must submit additional compliance reports or other requested information sworn under penalty of perjury, appear for depositions, and produce documents for inspection and copying
requirements.quoted_text: A. Within 14 days of receipt of aa written request from aa representative of the 99 Commission, Defendant must: submit additional compliance reports or other requested information, which must be sworn under penalty of perjury; appear for depositions; and produce documents, for inspection and copying. The Commission is also authorized to obtain discovery, without further leave of court, using any of the procedures prescribed by Federal Rules of Civil Procedure 29,30 (including telephonic depositions), 31, 33, 34, 36, 45, and 69.

requirements.description: Commission is authorized to communicate directly with Defendant and Defendant must permit Commission representatives to interview any employee or other person affiliated with Defendant who has agreed to such an interview
requirements.quoted_text: B. For matters concerning this Order, the Commission is authorized to communicate directly with Defendant. Defendant must permit representatives of the Commission to interview any employee or other person affiliated with any Defendant who has agreed to such an interview. The person interviewed may have counsel present.

requirements.description: Commission may use all other lawful means, including posing as consumers, suppliers, or other individuals or entities to Defendant or any affiliated individual or entity, without the necessity of identification or prior notice
requirements.quoted_text: C. The Commission may use all other lawful means, including posing, through its representatives, as consumers, suppliers, or other individuals or entities, to Defendant or any individual or entity affiliated with Defendant, without the necessity of identification or prior notice. Nothing in this Order limits the Commission's lawful use of compulsory process, pursuant to Sections 99 and 20 of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 49, 57b-1.

duration.termination_text: For 20 years following entry of this Order, Defendant must submit a compliance notice, sworn under penalty of perjury, within 14 days of any change... Defendant must create certain records for 20 years after entry of the Order... each 2 year period thereafter for 20 years after service of the Order for the biennial Assessments.

takeaway_brief: RockYou failed to secure 32 million email addresses and passwords, and knowingly collected personal data from approximately 179,000 children without parental consent in violation of COPPA.
takeaway_full: RockYou, Inc. failed to implement basic data security measures, resulting in unauthorized access to approximately 32 million users' email addresses and passwords, while also falsely claiming to use commercially reasonable safeguards. The company knowingly collected personal information from approximately 179,000 children under age 13 without parental consent or notice, in violation of COPPA, despite falsely representing it did not do so. The FTC order required civil penalty payment, enjoined future COPPA violations, required deletion of children's data, prohibited security misrepresentations, mandated an information security program with third-party assessments, and imposed compliance reporting.
